name: Voss Barn, North Edge of Sandusky, Shore Road
description: A weathered hay barn a quarter mile north of the dry goods store on the road toward the lake, its hayloft converted into a hidden rest space with blankets and a water barrel. This is the last station before the shore road, and seven people are currently inside it waiting for word.
atmosphericDetails: The sound of the lake is audible from the hayloft — the low chop of Erie in a northeast wind, closer than it seemed an hour ago.
Seven people in the hayloft: five adults and two children, the children asleep against each other under a single wool blanket.
A lantern hangs on a peg inside the barn door, unlit — it will be lit only as a signal that it is safe to move to the shore.

The ballast stones from the nearby rail spur are visible through the barn's east-facing gap in the siding — the iron horse's infrastructure is within a stone's throw of the network's most vulnerable point.
